canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, DBPedia is consistently characterized as a benchmark dataset derived from Wikipedia used to evaluate models. The prevailing usage of DBPedia is to measure `Text classification`, with most papers defining it as a topic or ontology classification task. This common operationalization involves categorizing text into one of 14 classes, as multiple sources specify it is for "14-way topic classification". For instance, one paper describes it as a task of "multi-class classification of Wikipedia articles". A distinct, less common framing is presented by one paper which describes DBPedia as having a "three-layer taxonomy tree, with 9/70/219 classes" and uses it to measure `Hierarchical text classification`. The dataset is also reported in this collection of papers as a tool for assessing `In-context and few-shot learning` and `Sensitivity`.

Large (Vision) Language Models are Unsupervised In-Context Learners
Plausible Token Amplification for Improving Accuracy of Differentially Private In-Context Learning Based on Implicit Bayesian Inference
The Ripple Effect: On Unforeseen Complications of Backdoor Attacks

definitions.text: A benchmark dataset for 14-way topic classification derived from Wikipedia, used to evaluate a model's ability to categorize text into one of fourteen ontological classes.
definitions.paper: Gen-Z: Generative Zero-Shot Text Classification with Contextualized Label Descriptions

definitions.text: The DBpedia ontology classification dataset, a benchmark for topic classification.
definitions.paper: In-Context Pretraining: Language Modeling Beyond Document Boundaries

definitions.text: A dataset for ontology classification derived from Wikipedia, used to evaluate text categorization.
definitions.paper: IDEAL: Influence-Driven Selective Annotations Empower In-Context Learners in Large Language Models

definitions.text: A topic classification dataset derived from Wikipedia, with 14 classes corresponding to DBpedia ontology types.
definitions.paper: A Systematic Analysis of Base Model Choice for Reward Modeling

relationships.evidence: We study text classification on three datasets: 4-way news classification AGNews (Zhang et al., 2015), 6-way question classification TREC (Voorhees and Tice, 2000), and 14-way topic classification DBPedia (Zhang et al., 2015).
